mapping.a_element: Z_4 spin lattice representation of the Ashkin-Teller model at the Fateev-Zamolodchikov point
fractal dimensions of interfaces in the Ashkin-Teller model
mapping.b_element: scaling and finite-size-scaling behavior (e.g., susceptibility, specific heat) in the 4-state Potts model

mapping.a_element: critical line described by Z_4 parafermionic theory (c=1)
mapping.b_element: critical behavior of the 4-state Potts model with logarithmic corrections

mapping.a_element: classification of primary operators of the parafermionic algebra
mapping.b_element: universal and non-universal logarithmic corrections to scaling

brief_justification: While both papers study critical phenomena in Z_4-symmetric models (Ashkin-Teller and 4-state Potts), their core mechanisms differ: Paper A focuses on geometric properties (fractal dimensions of interfaces) governed by parafermionic conformal field theory, whereas Paper B analyzes logarithmic corrections to thermodynamic scaling laws. The shared symmetry does not imply shared underlying machinery.
